record.preference_summary: Prefer fast autonomous execution on bounded approved Skeleton tasks while preserving explicit safety gates.

operating_rules.statement: Proceed through implementation, reconciliation, and verification when the task scope and approval boundary are already explicit.
Stay within established approval boundaries and preserve protected runtime, deployment, activation, and operator-gated actions.
Minimize issue churn by avoiding unnecessary confirmation loops and keeping public-safe preparation work focused on the approved task.
Status reports must include next-action and operator-action fields whenever reporting task state back for review.
Use safe read-only parallelization for inspection, comparison, and verification when it does not cross approval or privacy boundaries.
Use verified memory layers incrementally, preferring exact canonical facts first and treating derived layers as supporting context.
Canonical SQLite remains the authority for exact memory; candidate manifests do not activate records or change canonical storage.
Use Graphify only for derived code and relationship context unless exact canonical confirmation supports a memory change.
Use MemPalace semantic results as non-authoritative context that must not override canonical exact memory.
Require exact canonical confirmation before relying on derived Graphify or MemPalace evidence for any patch proposal.
